name: Mount Naranco Monuments and Finnish Track Loop
name_original: San Miguel de Lillo, Santa María del Naranco y la Pista Finlandesa — ruta circular
description: This difficult 6.3-mile jogging route on Monte Naranco combines historical sites like San Miguel de Lillo with panoramic views of Oviedo.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Oviedo, Spain, offer a diverse range of landscapes, from urban green spaces to challenging mountain trails. The city's location provides access to varied terrain, including river paths and the iconic Monte Naranco. This region is characterized by its network of greenways, accessible city parks, and the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. Best jogging routes around Oviedo The most popular jogging route is Winter Park to Manjoya via Vía Verde Oviedo-Fuso de la Reina, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail that takes 44 minutes to complete. This moderate path offers a pleasant experience along a dedicated greenway. Another top favourite among local runners is Pista Finlandesa and Oviedo City Loop, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path. This route combines urban sections with the popular Pista Finlandesa, known for its flat terrain. Local runners also love the Mount Naranco Monuments and Finnish Track Loop, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through the slopes of Monte Naranco, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Jogging around Oviedo is defined by mountain trails, river paths, and urban green spaces. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy urban jogs to more difficult mountain climbs. The routes in Oviedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 27 reviews. More than 2500 runners have used komoot to explore Oviedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available around Oviedo?
A: There are over 120 running routes available around Oviedo, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails to explore.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Oviedo?
A: The running routes in Oviedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 27 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from urban green spaces to challenging mountain trails, and the scenic views, especially from Monte Naranco.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Oviedo?
A: Yes, Oviedo offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For a pleasant urban experience, consider the trails within the city's green spaces. The region has 9 easy routes in total, providing gentle terrain for a relaxed run.

Q: Where can I find challenging running trails near Oviedo?
A: For a more challenging experience, head to Monte Naranco. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056079341/mount-naranco-monuments-and-finnish-track-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Naranco Monuments and Finnish Track Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 6.3-mile (10.1 km) trail that offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the city and its surroundings. There are 37 difficult routes in the area for experienced runners.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Oviedo?
A: Yes, Oviedo features several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056059710/campo-de-san-francisco-y-parque-de-invierno-circular-route-in-oviedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de San Francisco y Parque de Invierno — circular route in Oviedo&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the city's main parks for a refreshing urban jog.

Q: Can I find running paths with scenic viewpoints in Oviedo?
A: Absolutely. Monte Naranco is renowned for its scenic viewpoints, offering stunning vistas of Oviedo and the Asturian landscape. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056079341/mount-naranco-monuments-and-finnish-track-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Naranco Monuments and Finnish Track Loop&lt;/a&gt; provide excellent opportunities to enjoy these views while running.

Q: Are there any greenways or dedicated paths for running in Oviedo?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde Oviedo-Fuso de la Reina is a fantastic greenway for running. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056050433/winter-park-to-manjoya-via-via-verde-oviedo-fuso-de-la-reina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Winter Park to Manjoya via Vía Verde Oviedo-Fuso de la Reina&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 4.4-mile (7.1 km) trail that offers a consistent and pleasant surface for joggers.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the running routes near Oviedo?
A: While jogging near Oviedo, you can explore various natural attractions. The broader region offers impressive gorges, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/297709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Xanas&lt;/a&gt;, and fascinating caves like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covarones of the Tuernes River&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also feature river paths and mountain scenery.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running trails?
A: Yes, particularly on Monte Naranco, you can combine your run with visits to historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056079341/mount-naranco-monuments-and-finnish-track-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Naranco Monuments and Finnish Track Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by significant pre-Romanesque monuments like San Miguel de Lillo.

Q: Are the running routes in Oviedo accessible by public transport?
A: Many urban and peri-urban running routes in Oviedo are easily accessible by public transport. For routes starting within the city center or near major parks, local bus services often provide convenient access points. For specific routes, it's advisable to check local transport schedules.

Q: What is the Pista Finlandesa and why is it popular for running?
A: The Pista Finlandesa (Finnish Track) on Monte Naranco is a particularly popular spot for local runners. It's known for its relatively flat terrain and scenic vistas, offering a smooth and enjoyable running experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056070575/pista-finlandesa-and-oviedo-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pista Finlandesa and Oviedo City Loop&lt;/a&gt; incorporates this well-loved track.

Q: Are there any less crowded jogging spots near Oviedo?
A: While popular routes can see more traffic, exploring some of the longer mountain trails or lesser-known river paths further from the city center can offer a more secluded running experience. The vast network of 120 routes means there are always quieter options to discover.
tips: From here you have panoramic views of Oviedo.

The Palace of Ramiro I located at the foot of Mount Naranco is a unique construction without a clear purpose (palace, church, royal pavilion, royal hall, etc.). The building, with a rectangular floor plan, is divided into two floors, a lower one or crypt that is completely closed and an open upper one, joined by an external staircase.

Unfortunately, only a third of the original construction has come down to us, since the entire head and part of the naves collapsed, possibly in the 11th century, apparently due to land displacement caused by a nearby stream. At present, after the excavations carried out throughout the last century, we know not only the part that still exists, but also its entire original floor plan.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the San Miguel de Lillo, Santa María del Naranco y la Pista Finlandesa circular route?
faqs.answer: This challenging jogging route usually takes around 1 hour and 20 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your fitness level and how much time you spend enjoying the sights.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult, often described as a 'hard run' or 'carrera difícil'. It features a significant elevation gain of approximately 365 meters over 10.1 kilometers, including particularly steep sections like the 'cuesta de 1000 demonios'.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pista Finlandesa circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. Sections of the route, particularly the 'Pista Finlandesa', offer softer, more natural running paths, often wood-chipped. Other parts, especially leading to the historical monuments along the Avenida de Monumentos, may be paved. Be prepared for significant climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in historical and natural beauty. You'll pass two UNESCO World Heritage sites: the pre-Romanesque Saint Mary of Naranco and the Church of San Miguel de Lillo. Additionally, the ascent of Monte Naranco provides panoramic views of Oviedo and the surrounding mountains.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the San Miguel de Lillo, Santa María del Naranco y la Pista Finlandesa circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the route is located on Monte Naranco, accessible from Oviedo. Look for parking options near the base of Monte Naranco or along the Avenida de Monumentos, which leads up to the historical sites.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, public transport options are available from Oviedo to the Monte Naranco area, which can bring you close to the starting points for this route. Check local bus schedules for routes serving the monuments or the Pista Finlandesa.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for jogging. In winter, be mindful of potential colder conditions on Monte Naranco. The monuments are said to have an 'aura of mystery' even in foggy conditions.

faqs.question: Is the San Miguel de Lillo, Santa María del Naranco y la Pista Finlandesa route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails on Monte Naranco are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially around the historical sites and in areas with other visitors. Ensure you carry water for your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for Monte Naranco or the monuments?
faqs.answer: Access to Monte Naranco and the jogging trails themselves does not require a permit or entrance fee. However, there might be small fees or specific visiting hours if you wish to enter the interiors of the Saint Mary of Naranco or the Church of San Miguel de Lillo.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails on Monte Naranco?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with several other official trails on Monte Naranco. You'll find it overlaps with sections of the Ruta Xixon - Puertu Payares, Puente Viejo-Pico Paisano-Puente Gallegos-El Pedreru-San Pedro de Nora, and, of course, the Pista Finlandesa itself, among others.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or restaurants near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, given its proximity to Oviedo and popular historical sites, you can find cafes and restaurants in the vicinity of Monte Naranco, particularly closer to the city or along the main access roads to the monuments.

waypoints.name: Church of San Miguel de Lillo
